question: What KYC documents are required?
answer:
For institutions: legal entity registration, proof of address, UBO identification (passport copies), source of funds declaration, corporate structure chart, and authorized signatory list.

For individuals: passport copy, proof of address, source of funds declaration.

question: Can I upgrade from Starter Access to full institutional access?
answer: Yes. Once your portfolio reaches $100,000, contact your relationship manager or request an upgrade in your dashboard. Your limits will be increased automatically after verification.

question: How do I reset my password?
answer: Click "Forgot Password" on the login page. You will receive an email with a password reset link. Follow the instructions to create a new password.

question: How do I enable two-factor authentication (2FA)?
answer: Go to Account Settings → Security → Enable 2FA. Scan the QR code with Google Authenticator or Authy. Enter the verification code to confirm. We strongly recommend 2FA for all accounts.

question: I didn't receive my OTP code. What should I do?
answer: Check your spam folder. Wait 1-2 minutes. If still not received, click "Resend Code." If the issue persists, contact support at institutional@alfardanq9.com.

question: How do I update my KYC documents?
answer: Go to Account Settings → KYC Verification. Upload the new documents. Our compliance team will review within 24-48 hours.

question: Can I have multiple users on one account?
answer: Yes. We support sub-accounts for institutions. Contact your relationship manager to set up team access with role-based permissions (view only, trade, withdraw, admin).

question: How do I close my account?
answer: Withdraw all funds first. Then go to Account Settings → Close Account. You will be asked to confirm. Account closure is permanent.

question: How do I update my personal information?
answer: Go to Account Settings → Profile. Update your name, address, phone number, or email. Some changes may require KYC re-verification.

question: What happens if I fail KYC?
answer: Our compliance team will notify you of the specific issue. You may be asked to provide additional documents. If still not approved, you will not be able to use the platform.

question: Can I open an account from outside the UAE?
answer: Yes. Remote onboarding is available worldwide. You will complete KYC digitally. Some countries may have restrictions; contact support for specific questions.

question: Do you accept US persons?
answer: Please contact our compliance team directly for guidance on US person restrictions and requirements.

question: What is the minimum age to open an account?
answer: You must be at least 18 years old. Institutional accounts require authorized signatories who are at least 18.

question: How do I verify my email address?
answer: After registration, you will receive a verification email. Click the link inside. If you didn't receive it, check spam or click "Resend Verification Email" in your dashboard.

question: How do I verify my phone number?
answer: During registration, you will receive an SMS with a 6-digit code. Enter the code in the verification screen. If you don't receive it, click "Resend Code."

question: Can I change my email address?
answer: Yes. Go to Account Settings → Profile → Change Email. You will need to verify the new email address.

question: Can I change my phone number?
answer: Yes. Go to Account Settings → Profile → Change Phone Number. You will receive an OTP to verify the new number.

question: What is the difference between an individual and institutional account?
answer: Individual accounts are for HNWIs. Institutional accounts are for family offices, funds, and corporate entities. Institutional accounts have higher limits, dedicated relationship managers, and additional compliance requirements.

question: Can I verify your reserves myself?
answer: Yes. Truncated wallet addresses are shown in your dashboard. Full addresses and on-chain verification are available under NDA for institutional clients.

question: What is a margin call?
answer: A margin call occurs when your loan's collateral value drops close to the liquidation threshold. You will receive notifications via email and SMS. You have 24 hours to add more collateral or repay part of the loan.

question: What is liquidation?
answer: If you do not respond to a margin call and your LTV reaches the liquidation threshold (75% of LTV), your collateral will be sold to repay the loan. Any remaining funds are returned to you.

question: How do I avoid liquidation?
answer: Monitor your LTV in the dashboard. Set up alerts. Add more collateral when the market drops. Or repay part of the loan to lower your LTV.

question: What is the validator uptime SLA?
answer: We maintain a 99.95% validator uptime SLA backed by redundant infrastructure and 24/7 monitoring.

question: What happens if Fireblocks goes down?
answer: Assets are safely held in custody; access is restored when the service returns. The Lloyd's policy also covers such scenarios under the Technology E&O section.

question: Can I see my wallet addresses?
answer: Yes. In your client dashboard, you can view truncated wallet addresses for each asset. Full addresses are available under NDA.

question: Is my custody segregated?
answer: Yes. Every client's assets are held in fully segregated on-chain addresses with independent audit trails. We maintain a strict no-rehypothecation policy.

question: What is no-rehypothecation?
answer: Your assets are never lent, staked without consent, or used as collateral for other clients' positions. They remain exclusively yours.

question: What is the difference between hot and cold storage?
answer: Cold storage (95% of assets) is offline and not connected to the internet — maximum security. Warm storage (5% of assets) is online for operational liquidity.

question: How do I report a security issue?
answer:
Email security@alfardanq9.com. Do not post security issues in public chat or forums. We will respond within 24 hours.

⚡ CATEGORIE 3: STAKING (Vragen 46-65)

question: What staking do you offer?
answer: We offer institutional staking for Ethereum (ETH), Solana (SOL), Polygon (POL), Cardano (ADA), Polkadot (DOT), Avalanche (AVAX), and Cosmos (ATOM).

question: What is the current APY?
answer: Current APYs: ETH 5.2%, SOL 7.1%, POL 5.8%, ADA 4.5%, DOT 14.2%, AVAX 8.5%, ATOM 18.5%. Rates are subject to network conditions.

question: Is there a lock-up period?
answer: Most networks have flexible unstaking (no lock-up). Polkadot requires 28 days, Avalanche 14 days, Cosmos 21 days.

question: What is the minimum staking amount?
answer: Minimum staking is $100,000 per network. For larger allocations, we offer tiered pricing.

question: What are the staking tiers?
answer:
Standard ($1M+): Base APY rates, daily rewards, standard reporting.
Institutional ($5M+): +0.3% enhanced rates, priority validators, dedicated RM.
Sovereign ($25M+): +0.6% enhanced rates, bespoke compounding, direct desk access.

question: How do I unstake?
answer: Go to Staking → Active Positions → Select network → "Unstake." Unstaking periods vary by network.

question: What is slashing in staking?
answer: Slashing is a penalty imposed by the network for validator misbehavior. We maintain a slashing protection reserve that fully indemnifies clients.

question: Have you ever had a slashing event?
answer: No. Zero slashing events since inception.

question: Can I stake from my custody wallet?
answer: Yes. You can transfer funds from custody to staking instantly within the dashboard.

question: Can I stake from an external wallet?
answer: No. You must first deposit to your Al Fardan Q9 custody wallet, then stake from there.

question: What happens to my rewards?
answer: Rewards are automatically added to your custodial account daily. You can then restake, hold, or withdraw.

question: Can I auto-compound my rewards?
answer: Yes. Our proprietary compounding engine automatically re-stakes accrued rewards based on gas cost analysis. Backtesting shows 12-18% higher effective yield versus manual compounding.

question: Can I choose which validator to stake with?
answer: No. We use our own institutional validator fleet to ensure maximum security and performance. You stake with Al Fardan Q9, not individual validators.

question: What networks do you support?
answer: Ethereum, Solana, Polygon, Cardano, Polkadot, Avalanche, and Cosmos.

question: Can I stake more than one asset?
answer: Yes. You can stake any supported asset. Each network has its own staking position and rewards.

question: How do I track my staking rewards?
answer:
Go to Staking module → Reward History. You can view daily, monthly, and annual rewards. Export to CSV available.

💰 CATEGORIE 4: LENDING (Vragen 66-90)

question: What lending do you offer?
answer: We offer Bitcoin-backed and Ethereum-backed lending through our Private Heritage Vault. You pledge BTC or ETH as collateral and receive liquidity in USD, AED, or EUR.

question: What is the maximum LTV?
answer: Up to 75% LTV — among the highest in institutional crypto lending. Most competitors offer only 50-60%.

question: How does 75% LTV compare to competitors?
answer: Most institutional lenders offer 50-60% LTV. Al Fardan Q9 offers up to 75% — matching the best in the market (Figure, Bitget) and higher than Arch (60%), Ledn (50%), and Strike (50%).

question: What is the minimum pledge amount?
answer: Minimum pledge is 0.5 BTC or 10 ETH.

question: What are the lending tiers?
answer:
Starter (0.5-1 BTC): 4.9% APR
Core (1-5 BTC): 4.5% APR
Pro (5-10 BTC): 4.2% APR
Institutional (10+ BTC): 3.9% APR

question: What is the liquidation threshold?
answer: Liquidation occurs when your LTV reaches 75% of your original LTV. For a 75% LTV loan, liquidation starts when LTV exceeds 75%.

question: How do I add more collateral?
answer: Go to Lending → Active Loans → Select your loan → "Add Collateral." You can add more BTC or ETH at any time.

question: How long does disbursement take?
answer: After collateral is confirmed (3 network confirmations), funds are disbursed within 24-48 hours via wire transfer.

question: What currencies can I receive my loan in?
answer: USD, AED, or EUR. You choose at the time of application.

question: Can I take out multiple loans?
answer: Yes. You can have multiple active loans against different collateral or the same collateral (if total LTV remains within limits).

question: Can I use loan proceeds to buy more crypto?
answer: Yes. You can use the funds for any purpose, including buying more crypto on our platform or external exchanges.

question: What is the maximum loan amount?
answer: Based on your collateral value and LTV. Example: 1 BTC at $70,000 with 75% LTV = $52,500 maximum loan.

question: How is interest calculated?
answer: Daily on the outstanding loan balance. Payments are applied to interest first, then principal.

question: Can I make partial repayments?
answer: Yes. Partial repayments reduce your principal and lower your interest costs.

question: What happens if I miss a payment?
answer: We send reminders 7 days, 3 days, and 1 day before the due date. If missed, we contact you directly. Continued non-payment may result in liquidation.

question: Is my collateral safe during the loan?
answer: Yes. Your collateral is held in a segregated Fireblocks MPC wallet, fully insured by Lloyd's of London.

question: Can I withdraw my collateral early?
answer: Only after fully repaying the loan. Partial collateral withdrawal is not allowed.

question: What happens to my collateral if I default?
answer: The collateral is liquidated to repay the loan. Any surplus after repayment is returned to you.

question: How do I check my loan status?
answer:
Go to Lending module → Active Loans. You can see LTV, collateral value, interest accrued, and next payment date.

📊 CATEGORIE 5: OTC DESK (Vragen 91-110)

question: What is the minimum OTC trade size?
answer: Minimum trade size is $100,000 USD. Maximum is $50M+ per trade (larger sizes available upon request).

question: What assets can I trade?
answer: We support 16+ digital assets including BTC, ETH, SOL, USDT, USDC, BNB, XRP, ADA, AVAX, DOT, MATIC, LINK, ATOM, UNI, AAVE, and ARB.

question: What currencies can I settle in?
answer: Direct settlement in AED, SAR, KWD, BHD, QAR, OMR, USD, and EUR.

question: How do I get a quote?
answer: Contact our OTC desk via website chat, WhatsApp, or email. Our desk will provide a firm quote valid for 15 minutes.

question: Who is my contact?
answer: Your dedicated relationship manager is Layla Al-Fayez, Head of Institutional OTC Desk. You can reach her via Signal, WhatsApp, or encrypted email.

question: What is the fee / spread?
answer: Spreads start from 0.05% – 0.25% depending on asset and trade size. No commission. All fees are disclosed prior to execution.

question: How do I get a firm quote?
answer: Provide the asset, side (buy/sell), and notional amount. You will receive a firm quote within 2 minutes, valid for 15 minutes.

question: What is the one-click settlement link?
answer: After confirming the trade, you receive a unique, single-use link to finalize settlement. The link expires in 15 minutes for security.

question: Can I trade during weekends?
answer: Yes. Our OTC desk is available 24/7, 365 days a year.

question: What is the process for an OTC trade?
answer: Contact desk → 2. Receive firm quote (15-min validity) → 3. Confirm trade → 4. Execute → 5. Settle (crypto 1-4 hrs, fiat 24 hrs)

question: Can I get a quote in AED or SAR?
answer: Yes. We provide firm quotes in AED, SAR, KWD, BHD, QAR, OMR, USD, and EUR.

question: Do you offer algorithmic execution?
answer: Yes. For large trades, we use smart order routing across 50+ liquidity providers to achieve best price with minimal market impact.

question: What is the price slippage guarantee?
answer: Zero slippage for all OTC trades. The price you confirm is the price you get.

question: Can I trade options or derivatives?
answer: Not currently. We offer spot OTC trading only.

question: How do I contact the OTC desk after hours?
answer: The desk is available 24/7 via website chat, WhatsApp, and email. Phone support is available for emergency trades.

question: What information do I need to provide for a quote?
answer: Asset, side (buy/sell), notional amount, and your preferred settlement currency.

question: Can I cancel a trade after confirming?
answer: No. Once confirmed, the trade is binding. The one-click link expires after 15 minutes if not used.

question: What happens after I click the settlement link?
answer: The trade is executed immediately. You receive a confirmation email. Settlement proceeds are sent within the stated timeframes.

question: How do I track my trade history?
answer:
Go to OTC Desk module → Trade History. All past trades are listed with status and settlement details.

🔐 CATEGORIE 6: CUSTODY (Vragen 111-130)

question: What custody technology do you use?
answer: We use Fireblocks MPC-CMP (Multi-Party Computation with Configurable Multi-Protocol) technology — the same institutional-grade custody used by the world's largest banks and exchanges.

question: Where are my assets held?
answer: 95% of assets are held in air-gapped cold storage across multiple Tier IV data centers in separate jurisdictions. 5% in warm wallets for operational liquidity.

question: How do I deposit to my custody wallet?
answer: Go to Custody module → Deposit. Select the asset. Copy the deposit address or scan the QR code. Send funds from your external wallet.

question: How do I withdraw funds?
answer: Go to Dashboard → Select Asset → Click "Withdraw." Enter the destination wallet address and amount. Confirm with 2FA.

question: Why is my withdrawal pending?
answer: Withdrawals are subject to security review. Most are processed automatically. "Pending Review" means compliance needs to verify the transaction.

question: What is the withdrawal fee?
answer: No withdrawal fees for on-platform transfers. Network gas fees are passed through at cost.

question: Can I transfer funds between custody and staking/lending?
answer: Yes. Use the "Transfer" feature in your dashboard. Transfers are instant and free.

question: What is the minimum deposit?
answer: No minimum deposit for custody. However, to use staking or lending, minimums apply ($100,000 for staking, 0.5 BTC for lending).

question: What is the custody fee?
answer: Annual custody fee: 0.10% – 0.50% of AUM depending on volume. No setup fees. Institutional volume discounts available.

question: Can I get a custody-only account?
answer: Yes. You can use custody services without using staking or lending.

question: Do you support hardware wallet integration?
answer: Not directly. However, you can withdraw to any external wallet, including hardware wallets.

question: What blockchains do you support for custody?
answer: 40+ blockchain protocols including Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, BNB Chain, Polygon, Cardano, Polkadot, Avalanche, Cosmos, and others.

question: Can I stake directly from custody?
answer: Yes. Use the "Transfer" feature to move funds from custody to staking instantly.

question: Can I use my custody wallet as collateral for lending?
answer: Yes. You can use funds in your custody wallet as collateral for loans without moving them.

question: How do I generate a deposit address?
answer:
Go to Custody module → Deposit → Select asset → "Generate Address." Use this address for each deposit.

💼 CATEGORIE 7: INVESTMENT ADVICE & STRATEGY (Vragen 131-150)

question: Can you help me with an investment?
answer: We can guide you through our platform and explain how our products work. However, we do not provide personal investment advice. You make your own decisions based on your research and risk tolerance.

question: Is this a good time to buy Bitcoin?
answer: We do not provide market timing advice. Digital assets are volatile. Do your own research, consult a financial advisor, and only invest what you can afford to lose.

question: What is the best strategy for staking?
answer: Most institutional clients start with a diversified mix of ETH, SOL, and DOT. Flexible unstaking for most networks. Tiered pricing offers enhanced rates for larger commitments.

question: How much yield can I expect?
answer: Current APYs: ETH 5.2%, SOL 7.1%, POL 5.8%, ADA 4.5%, DOT 14.2%, AVAX 8.5%, ATOM 18.5%. Network-dependent and subject to change.

question: Is crypto safe for my family office?
answer: Digital assets carry risks including volatility, regulatory changes, and technology risks. Al Fardan Q9 provides institutional-grade security: Fireblocks MPC, $250M Lloyd's insurance, VARA-licensed infrastructure, and full asset segregation.

question: What is the minimum investment for a family office?
answer: Standard minimum is $100,000. For smaller portfolios ($3,000 – $99,999), we offer Starter Access. Apply through your dashboard.

question: Can you manage my crypto portfolio?
answer: No. Al Fardan Q9 is an execution-only platform. You maintain full control. We provide infrastructure, security, and execution. You provide the strategy.

question: Can I set up recurring buys?
answer: Yes. Go to Dashboard → Recurring Buys. Set frequency (daily, weekly, monthly), amount, and asset.

question: What is the difference between staking and lending?
answer: Staking earns yield by securing proof-of-stake networks. Lending provides liquidity against your collateral (BTC/ETH). Both generate yield, but with different risk profiles.

question: What is the risk of lending?
answer: Main risks: collateral price drops leading to liquidation. We provide margin call warnings (24 hours) and low LTV options (starting at 10%).

question: What is the risk of custody?
answer: Main risks: theft, hacking, private key loss. We use Fireblocks MPC, cold storage, and $250M Lloyd's insurance.

question: What is the risk of OTC trading?
answer: Main risks: counterparty settlement risk. We use Fireblocks-powered settlement with 1-4 hour crypto settlement.

question: Can I lose all my money?
answer: Yes. Digital assets are volatile and carry risk. Only invest what you can afford to lose. We provide security, but market risk remains.

question: What is the historical return of Bitcoin?
answer: Bitcoin has been the best-performing asset of the last decade but has also seen 80% drawdowns. Past performance does not guarantee future results.

question: What is the historical return of Ethereum?
answer: Ethereum has also seen significant returns and volatility. Staking rewards add yield on top of price appreciation.

question: Should I stake ETH or hold it?
answer: Staking generates yield (5.2% APY) but may have unstaking delays. Holding provides full liquidity. The choice depends on your liquidity needs.

question: Should I use leverage?
answer:
We do not recommend leverage for most investors. Our lending product is overcollateralized (LTV up to 75%), which is different from leveraged trading.

📊 CATEGORIE 8: REAL-WORLD ASSETS (RWA) – Airbus & Gold (Vragen 151-170)

question: What real-world assets are coming to Al Fardan Q9?
answer: Coming soon: Airbus A320 aircraft leasing and tokenized gold bars. Both are for qualified investors only. You can join the waiting list now.

question: What is the Airbus A320 investment?
answer: An airline is raising capital to expand its fleet with a pre-selected Airbus A320 passenger aircraft. Target IRR: 20%. Minimum investment: $20,000 USD. Maturity: 62 months. Early exit via secondary market.

question: What are the gold bars investment details?
answer: Tokenized 1 kg gold bars by MULTIMET, a licensed Abu Dhabi metal broker. Gold stored at BRINKS UAE facility. Minimum investment: $88,000 USD. Redemption in kind or secondary market.

question: How do I join the waiting list?
answer:
Complete KYC (qualified investor verification)

Go to Investments → Upcoming Offerings

Click "Join Waiting List" for Airbus A320 or Gold Bars

Receive email notification when offering launches

question: Can I pay with crypto for these investments?
answer: Yes. Only BTC and ETH are accepted. No stablecoins, no fiat. You can pay from your Al Fardan Q9 custody wallet or external wallet.

question: How is the BTC/ETH amount calculated?
answer: The investment amount is fixed in USD ($20,000 for Airbus, $88,000 for Gold). At subscription, the system calculates the equivalent BTC or ETH based on live market price.

question: What is the minimum investment in BTC/ETH?
answer: Airbus: ~0.2857 BTC or ~8 ETH (at $70k BTC, $2.5k ETH). Gold: ~1.2571 BTC or ~35.2 ETH. Actual amounts vary with market price.

question: Can I use both BTC and ETH for one investment?
answer: No. Each subscription must be paid entirely in either BTC or ETH — not a combination.

question: Can I pay from my Al Fardan Q9 custody wallet?
answer: Yes. Select "Pay from my Al Fardan Q9 wallet" → Choose BTC or ETH → Confirm. Instant transfer, no network fees.

question: Can I pay from an external wallet?
answer: Yes. Select "Pay with external wallet." You receive a unique deposit address for BTC or ETH. Send the exact amount. After 3 confirmations, your investment is processed.

question: What is the secondary market?
answer: A platform to sell your tokens to other investors before maturity. Features scheduled auctions, issuer-led buybacks, and institutional buyers for larger holdings.

question: Can I exit before maturity?
answer: Yes. Use the secondary market. Settlement typically within 1-3 business days after auction close.

question: Is the gold insured?
answer: Yes. Gold is stored at BRINKS UAE facility with full insurance coverage. Details in the offering memorandum.

question: Is the aircraft leasing Sharia-compliant?
answer: Currently under Sharia review. Decision will be announced before the offering opens.

question: What happens if the offering is oversubscribed?
answer: Waiting list members receive priority. Allocations are first-come, first-served within the waiting list.

question: How do I know if I qualify as a qualified investor?
answer: Net worth > $1M (excluding primary residence), or annual income > $200k ($300k joint), or hold a qualified investor certificate, or represent a licensed financial institution.

question: What other RWAs are coming?
answer: Cargo vessel leasing (15% IRR, min $20,000), BTC mining debt (16% p.a., min $20,000), Murabaha security (10% p.a., min 1,000 BHD). No waiting list needed for these.

question: How do I get notified when offerings launch?
answer:
Waiting list members receive email notifications. You can also check the Investments section in your dashboard.

🔧 CATEGORIE 9: TECHNICAL & API (Vragen 171-185)

question: Do you have an API?
answer: Yes. We offer a REST API for institutional clients with real-time market data, trading execution, and account management. API documentation is available upon request.

question: How do I get API access?
answer: Contact your relationship manager. API access is available for Institutional and Sovereign tier clients.

question: What is the average execution latency?
answer: Our matching engine provides <50ms average execution latency for institutional trades.

question: What blockchains do you support?
answer: 40+ blockchain protocols including Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, BNB Chain, Polygon, Cardano, Polkadot, Avalanche, Cosmos, and others.

question: Can I integrate with my existing systems?
answer: Yes. Our API integrates with major portfolio management systems, CRMs, and accounting software.

question: Is there a sandbox environment for testing?
answer: Yes. We provide a testnet environment for API development and testing. Contact support for access.

question: What is the rate limit for the API?
answer: Standard rate limit: 100 requests per minute. Higher limits available for institutional clients.

question: Do you have WebSocket for real-time data?
answer: Yes. WebSocket streams for live prices, order book, and trade execution status.

question: What data can I access via API?
answer: Account balances, transaction history, staking positions, lending status, OTC quotes, market data.

question: Is the API documented?
answer: Yes. Full API documentation with code examples is available in the Developer Portal.

question: Do you have a mobile app?
answer: Our platform is mobile-responsive and works in any browser. A native mobile app is planned for Q3 2026.

question: What is the system uptime guarantee?
answer: 99.9% platform uptime with 24/7 monitoring and redundant infrastructure.

question: How do I report a technical issue?

question: Do you support Web3 wallet connection?

Not directly. You deposit from external wallets to your custody wallet. Self-custody options are available for some products.

question: What are your support hours?

question: What is the emergency contact number?

question: How do I request an audit report?
